title: ageratina pichinchensis var. bustamenta
intro: Ageratina pichinchensis var. bustamenta, also known as the Ecuadorian mountain mint, is a plant species native to the high-altitude regions of Ecuador. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include relief from fever, colds, and digestive problems, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Traditionally, Ageratina pichinchensis var. bustamenta is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are prepared by steeping the leaves in hot water or mixing them with other herbs to create a medicinal concoction.
